title: Add Tribe Availability Enquiry Form with Results (Step 1 + Step 2)

requirements.summary: Add 'Find Available Crew' CTA button to Tribe Availability card, visible only for authenticated Directors and Production Houses
acceptanceCriteria: The CTA button 'Find Available Crew' appears inside the Tribe Availability card on the Landing Page
Button is only active/visible for authenticated users with account type Director or Production House
Non-qualifying users see a disabled button or tooltip explaining access is restricted to Directors and Production Houses
Clicking the button opens the enquiry form (inline within the section or as a modal)

file_operations.description: Add 'Find Available Crew' CTA button inside the Tribe Availability feature card (the card with calendar icon). The button must be conditionally rendered: visible and enabled only for authenticated users whose professionalDesignation is 'director' or whose account is a production house. For non-qualifying users, show the button in a disabled state with a tooltip explaining the restriction. On click, toggle inline enquiry form display within the card section. Use the authorization component to verify user permissions before showing the active button.

requirements.summary: Build Tribe Availability enquiry form with all required fields and conditional logic for Director/Production House
acceptanceCriteria: All 8 form fields are present and functional
Director Name field shows only when account type is Director; Production House Name shows only when account type is Production House
Technicians Needed field is a multi-select dropdown with the full predefined roles list; selected roles appear as removable tags/chips
Shoot Dates accepts a start and end date
Available From is a single date picker
All required fields are validated before submission

file_operations.description: Create the Tribe Availability enquiry form component with the following controlled form fields: (1) Project Name (text input, required), (2) Director Name (text input, shown only when user's professionalDesignation is 'director'), (3) Production House Name (text input, shown only when user's account type is production house), (4) Shoot City (text input, required), (5) Shoot Dates (date range picker with start and end date inputs, required), (6) Available From (single date picker, required), (7) Technicians Needed (multi-select dropdown using predefined roles: Cinematographer, Editor, Gaffer, Sound Recordist, Art Director, Colourist, Production Designer, Costume Designer, Makeup Artist, Stunt Coordinator, VFX Supervisor, Drone Operator, Focus Puller, Clapper Loader, Script Supervisor; selected roles displayed as removable tags, required), (8) Special Requirements (textarea, optional). Include form validation and a Submit/Search button. On submit, call a custom mutation hook to invoke the backend searchTechnicians function with the form data, then pass results up to the parent component via callback. Use date helper utilities from frontend/src/utils/dateHelpers.ts to convert Date objects to Time (bigint) for the backend.

Add a new mutation hook useSearchTechnicians that accepts TechnicianSearchInput and calls actor.searchTechnicians(searchInput). Return the TechnicianSearchResult including sameCityMatches and otherCityMatches arrays. Handle loading, error, and success states using React Query mutation.

requirements.summary: Display search results inline below enquiry form, split into 'Same City' and 'Other Cities' sections
acceptanceCriteria: After form submission, results appear below the form without navigating away
Results are split into 'Same City' and 'Other Cities' sections with clear headings
Each result card displays: name, designation, city, contact number
Empty state message shown if no matches found in a category
A loading/spinner state is shown while the query is in progress
User can modify the form and resubmit to get updated results

file_operations.description: Create the results display component that accepts TechnicianSearchResult as a prop. Render two sections: 'Same City' and 'Other Cities', each with a clear heading. For each section, map over the respective array (sameCityMatches or otherCityMatches) and display a card for each technician showing name, role (designation), city, and contactNumber. If a section has zero results, show an appropriate empty state message (e.g., 'No technicians found in your city' or 'No technicians found in other cities'). Include a loading/spinner state when the mutation is pending. Style the cards consistently with the AD TRIBE design system.

Wire the TribeAvailabilityEnquiryForm and TribeAvailabilityResults components into the Tribe Availability card section. When the 'Find Available Crew' button is clicked, display the enquiry form inline below the button. After form submission, pass the returned search results to the TribeAvailabilityResults component and display it inline below the form within the same card section. Manage form visibility and results state within the LandingPage component. Ensure the user can modify the form and resubmit to get updated results without navigating away.
